steps.title: Fit the table first
steps.content: This is a linear table question, so use two table points to find the slope and intercept. The table includes $(-6,65)$ and $(6,29)$, so $$m=\frac{29-65}{6-(-6)}=\frac{-36}{12}=-3$$ Use $y=mx+b$ with $(-6,65)$: $$65=-3(-6)+b=18+b$$ $$b=47$$

steps.title: Convert to the choice format
steps.content: The table line is $$y=-3x+47$$ Move terms to match the answer choices: $$3x+y=47$$ Multiplying by $3$ gives $$9x+3y=141$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice A is exactly $9x+3y=141$. Choice B has the same left side but the wrong constant. Choices C and D switch the coefficients to $3x+9y$, which does not match the fitted line. Therefore A is correct.
